criteria.requirement: Every criterion states a testable acceptance condition: an observable behaviour with a command or artifact that would demonstrate it.
criteria.pass: Each criterion names a concrete, checkable outcome a reviewer could confirm from the built repository.
criteria.fail: A criterion is aspirational or vague ('works well', 'is robust') with no observable pass condition.

criteria.requirement: Every planned slice traces to at least one criterion, and every criterion is covered by at least one slice.
criteria.pass: The slice/criterion mapping is total in both directions — no orphan slice, no uncovered criterion.
criteria.fail: A slice implements behaviour no criterion demands, or a criterion has no slice that satisfies it.

criteria.requirement: Requirements are mutually consistent and buildable with the stated interfaces — no criterion contradicts another.
criteria.pass: The criteria can all hold simultaneously in one implementation.
criteria.fail: Two criteria demand incompatible behaviour, or a criterion requires an interface another criterion forbids.

criteria.requirement: Scope is coherent and complete: every load-bearing assumption the build depends on is stated in the artifact.
criteria.pass: A builder could implement the whole scope from the artifact alone, with no unstated dependency.
criteria.fail: The artifact leans on an assumption — a file, a contract, a precondition — it never states.

criteria.requirement: Nothing in the artifact contradicts anything else in it — definitions, names, and constraints are used consistently throughout.
criteria.pass: Every term and constraint carries one meaning across the whole artifact.
criteria.fail: A term is defined one way and used another, or a constraint is asserted and later violated.
